examples.name: diversity_keeps_lower_scoring_story
examples.scenario: If three war-topic clusters land within a few points of each other, the service can reject one under the topic soft cap and keep a slightly lower-scoring economy cluster for diversity.

examples.name: low_score_story_rejected
examples.scenario: A commentary-like cluster below the editorial threshold is rejected even when capacity remains.

examples.name: regional_preference_breaks_near_tie
examples.scenario: Two near-tie clusters are both selectable, and the user wants local coverage with Iasi County as the regional anchor, so the regional story is preferred over a close national story.
examples.expected_decision_note: regional_preference_used=true for cluster-iasi-001 because it matched the user region in a near-tie.

examples.name: domain_preference_breaks_near_tie
examples.scenario: Two near-tie clusters remain after higher-scoring stories, and the user preference leans toward sport, so the sport cluster is preferred over a close-scoring politics cluster.

examples.name: stronger_story_still_wins_over_regional_preference
examples.scenario: A clearly stronger national or international cluster stays selected even when the listener asked for local coverage around Iasi County, because regional preference only acts as a soft near-tie signal.
examples.expected_decision_note: regional_preference_used=false for the winning stronger story because score lead was outside the near-tie tolerance.
